generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance GantryMotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Motorized actuators (servo/stepper motors) for horizontal/vertical movement of the gantry crane's articulated arms"
]

instance PIDController : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Positional control unit implementing PID feedback loops for precise positioning of the crane's puck-handling mechanism"
]

instance EmergencyStopMechanism : DTDFVocab:Actuator [
    base:desc "Safety mechanism for emergency halting of crane operations via Ethernet/Wi-Fi interface"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Rotational position sensors (encoders) tracking joint angles and linear actuator positions"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionSensorData
]

instance LoadCellSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Strain gauge load sensors monitoring payload weight and structural integrity"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LoadSensorData
]

instance KinematicSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "LIDAR/optical sensors detecting puck/container positions and spatial relationships"
    DTDFVocab:producedData KinematicSensorData
]

instance EnvironmentalSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded temperature/humidity sensors monitoring operational environment conditions"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalData
]

instance ActuatorFeedbackSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-frequency (kHz) feedback sensors monitoring actuator velocity and position"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuatorFeedbackData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance PositionSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoder
]

instance LoadSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LoadCellSensor
]

instance KinematicSensorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom KinematicSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalSensor
]

instance ActuatorFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ActuatorFeedbackSensor
]

instance MQTTMessageStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionSensorData, LoadSensorData, KinematicSensorData, EnvironmentalData, ActuatorFeedbackData
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance KinematicModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Forward/backward/inverse kinematic model derived from CAD representations (Fusion360/SolidWorks) simulating joint angles and motion dynamics"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABSimulinkModel
]

instance MotionDynamicsModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based model implementing inverse dynamics for actuator control optimization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MATLABControlSystemToolbox
]

instance HistoricalDataStore : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database (TimescaleDB) storing high-frequency crane operation data (position, velocity, torque) for historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTMessageStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PostgreSQLDatabase
]

instance RealTimeTrajectory : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Current operational trajectory data derived from real-time sensor streams for validation purposes"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTMessageStream
]

instance CADModelRepresentation : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "3D geometric model created from CAD tools (Fusion360) for visualization and spatial analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ThreeJSVisualization
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Message Queuing Telemetry Transport broker facilitating lightweight, event-driven communication between physical sensors and digital twin services"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataLoggingService, ContinuousValidationService
]

instance TimescaleDB : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Time-series database optimized for high-frequency data ingestion and real-time querying of crane operation metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataStore
]

instance MATLABSimulinkModel : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation environment (MATLAB/Simulink) for offline testing and validation of kinematic models"
    DTDFVocab:enables KinematicModel
]

instance MATLABControlSystemToolbox : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB toolbox for control system modeling and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotionDynamicsModel
]

instance PostgreSQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Scalable relational database for structured storage of historical operational data and metadata"
    DTDFVocab:enables HistoricalDataStore
]

instance ThreeJSVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "WebGL-based visualization framework for interactive 3D/2D rendering of crane states and trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationDashboard
]

instance PythonDataProcessing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python libraries (numpy, pandas) for data preprocessing, filtering, and normalization before transmission"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataPreprocessingService
]

instance DarTwinDocumentation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "DarTwin notation system for documenting system evolution and maintaining traceability between physical and digital states"
    DTDFVocab:enables SystemEvolutionTracking
]

// C6: Services
instance DynamicTrajectoryPlanning : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time algorithm optimizing crane motion paths to minimize energy consumption while avoiding collisions"
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimalPathInsight
]

instance FaultMitigationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous validation service detecting and compensating for actuator/sensor faults via residual analysis and ML-based anomaly detection"
    DTDFVocab:provides FaultDetectionAction
]

instance HistoricalDataLogging : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "High-frequency data capture service storing operational metrics (position, velocity, torque) into centralized database"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalAnalysisInsight
]

instance RealTimeVisualization : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard providing 3D/2D visualizations of crane state, trajectory, and load distribution"
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalStatusInsight
]

instance ContinuousValidation : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "State consistency checking service comparing predicted vs. actual joint angles with 5% deviation threshold"
    DTDFVocab:provides ValidationConfidence
]

instance DataPreprocessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Noise reduction and normalization service processing raw sensor data before transmission"
    DTDFVocab:provides CleanDataInsight
]

instance SystemEvolutionTracking : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Documentation service maintaining DarTwin-based traceability between physical and digital system states"
    DTDFVocab:provides EvolutionMetadata
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OptimalPathInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized motion trajectory reducing energy consumption while maintaining safe operational parameters"
]

instance FaultDetectionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic compensation for detected faults via actuator reconfiguration or emergency stop activation"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance HistoricalAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Post-processing analysis of historical operational data enabling control strategy validation"
]

instance OperationalStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of crane's physical state including position, trajectory, and load distribution"
]

instance ValidationConfidence : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Confidence measure indicating 5% deviation between digital and physical state representations"
]

instance CleanDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Processed sensor data with noise reduction and normalized values for reliable twin synchronization"
]

instance EvolutionMetadata : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "DarTwin documentation maintaining traceability of system evolution between physical and digital states"
]

// C1: Environment
instance PhysicalCraneEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains GantryMotorActuator, PIDController, EmergencyStopMechanism,
        PositionEncoder, LoadCellSensor, KinematicSensor, EnvironmentalSensor,
        ActuatorFeedbackSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains KinematicModel, MotionDynamicsModel, HistoricalDataStore,
        RealTimeTrajectory, CADModelRepresentation, MQTTBroker, TimescaleDB,
        MATLABSimulinkModel, MATLABControlSystemToolbox, PostgreSQLDatabase,
        ThreeJSVisualization, PythonDataProcessing, DarTwinDocumentation
]

instance CombinedSystemEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PhysicalCraneEnvironment, DigitalTwinEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual_to_physical_interaction** involves the digital twin sending control commands and validation feedback to the **Physical Crane** through the **Physical Crane Interface**. Key components include:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Movement commands**: Precise positional/velocity commands for the crane’s puck (container) transport, generated by the **Trajectory Generator Service** to minimize swinging motion.
   - **Trajectory execution**: Commands are issued in real-time to the crane’s controllers, ensuring optimal path adherence.

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - Commands are formatted as structured payloads (e.g., JSON or binary) via MQTT, adhering to the broker’s protocol for reliable delivery.
   - The **Control Application** (Python) translates digital commands into the crane’s proprietary control language (e.g., PLC or motion controller commands).

3. **Validation and Authentication**:
   - The **Continuous Validation Service** compares simulated trajectories (from the **Simulation Service**) with executed trajectories, triggering alerts if discrepancies exceed thresholds (e.g., >10% deviation).
   - **Feedback loops**: The **Human Machine Interface (HMI)** allows operators to override commands or adjust parameters, with validation ensuring commands are within operational limits.

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - The **Logging Application** records executed commands and their outcomes, enabling post-hoc analysis.
   - The **Dashboard** visualizes real-time feedback (e.g., error rates, trajectory deviations) to operators for immediate intervention.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - Operator overrides take precedence over automated commands, with the **HMI** mediating conflicts via priority rules (e.g., emergency stops overriding trajectories).
   - The **Continuous Validation Service** enforces safety thresholds, halting operations if critical parameters (e.g., load capacity) are breached.

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - The **Physical Crane Interface** acknowledges command receipt and execution status, ensuring traceability in the **Database**.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
  The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar employs **real-time behavioral adaptation** with **synchronized updates** between the physical greenhouse and its digital twin, leveraging **InfluxDB** for time-series data storage and **ActiveMQ** for message broker synchronization. While exact synchronization frequencies are not specified, the system appears designed for **low-latency adaptation**, likely in the **millisecond to sub-second range**, given its focus on self-adaptive behaviors. The **pre-recorded virtual machine data** suggests batch processing capabilities for historical twinning, but real-time operational synchronization is prioritized.

  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar uses **Azure-based tools** for runtime visualization, simulation, and prediction, implying **high-frequency data ingestion** (likely **1–10 Hz** for sensor data) to maintain temporal coherence between the Raspberry-Pi sensor network and the AutomationML-based digital twin. The system supports **real-time air quality monitoring** with **prediction horizons** (e.g., 15–60 minutes) for virus spread mitigation, suggesting **latency requirements under 1 second** for critical decision-making applications.

  **Not in Document** for the lab-scale gantry crane system regarding explicit twinning time scales, synchronization frequencies, or latency requirements.
  "
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "
  The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar adopts a **self-adaptive constellation** where the digital twin and physical system **co-evolve** through **behavioral adaptation loops**, using **InfluxDB + ActiveMQ** for real-time data exchange. The **orchestration** is **ad-hoc**, relying on **custom scripting** (likely Python/Java) to manage twinning logic, with **no commercial framework** integration. Resource management is handled via **virtual machine (VM) provisioning** with pre-recorded data, ensuring low-cost scalability.

  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar implements a **Model-Driven Digital Twin (MDDT) constellation**, leveraging **AutomationML** as the **ontology layer** for semantic interoperability between sensors, simulation engines, and visualization tools. The **orchestration** is **Azure-centric**, with **three example applications** (runtime visualization, simulation, prediction) executed in a **pipeline workflow** (e.g., sensor → Azure ML → prediction → dashboard). The **component integration** uses **Azure IoT Hub** for data ingestion, **Azure Functions** for event-driven processing, and **Azure Stream Analytics** for real-time analytics, ensuring **end-to-end coordination** without explicit twin-specific orchestration patterns.

  The **lab-scale gantry crane** system is depicted using the **C4 Model**, suggesting a **containerized constellation** where the digital twin is **orchestrated via Docker/Kubernetes** for modular deployment. The **architecture pattern** is **microservices-based**, with **component integration** via **REST/gRPC APIs** between the crane’s physical actuators, sensor network, and digital twin logic. **Resource management** is handled via **containerized VMs** (as implied by the C4 Model’s applicability), ensuring **scalable, decoupled execution** of twinning services.

  **Not in Document** for explicit mention of orchestration patterns beyond containerization in the lab-scale gantry crane.
  "
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar demonstrates a **self-adaptive digital twin lifecycle** focused on **real-time behavioral adaptation** of both the physical greenhouse and its digital counterpart. While the documents do not explicitly outline formal lifecycle stages (e.g., design, deployment, operation, maintenance), the exemplar leverages **pre-recorded virtual machine (VM) images** with embedded data for rapid prototyping and testing. The lifecycle governance appears **ad-hoc**, relying on **containerization/virtualization** (via VMs) and **InfluxDB/ActiveMQ** for dynamic data synchronization, implying iterative development and runtime adjustments. The absence of structured lifecycle documentation suggests a **development-first approach**, where the digital twin evolves alongside the physical system through **real-time sensor feedback integration** and **adaptive algorithms**.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar, while not detailing explicit lifecycle phases, incorporates a **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** pipeline with **AutomationML-based digital twin models**, suggesting a lifecycle that integrates **simulation, prediction, and runtime visualization** as core phases. Microsoft Azure tools enable **cloud-based scalability**, implying a **hybrid lifecycle** where offline modeling (Azure) transitions to real-time deployment (Raspberry Pi sensors). The lifecycle governance appears **framework-agnostic**, relying on **Azure’s versioning and deployment pipelines** for evolution management.

The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** (implied by the C4 Model context) does not provide explicit lifecycle details, but the C4 Model suggests a **software-centric lifecycle** where the digital twin’s architecture is visualized through **context/container diagrams**, implying iterative design and deployment phases. The absence of lifecycle governance specifics suggests a **domain-specific, ad-hoc approach** tied to **C4 Model compliance** for architecture documentation.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** system addresses fidelity and validity through:
- **Model fidelity**: The **self-adaptive digital twin** dynamically adjusts its behavioral models to match the physical greenhouse’s state, ensuring high fidelity in real-time operations.
- **Validation/verification**: Implicitly achieved via **closed-loop adaptation**—the digital twin’s adjustments are validated against real-world sensor data, though no explicit methods (e.g., Monte Carlo simulations) are described.
- **Uncertainty quantification**: Not detailed, but the use of **pre-recorded VM images** suggests initial calibration with historical data to mitigate drift.
- **Error detection/correction**: Likely implemented via **ActiveMQ’s message validation** and **InfluxDB’s data integrity checks**, though specifics are absent.
- **Quality assurance**: The **ad-hoc approach** implies manual oversight, but the system’s modularity (e.g., containerized components) facilitates modular updates.

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar ensures fidelity and validity via:
- **Model fidelity**: **AutomationML-based models** represent air quality dynamics (e.g., dispersion, ventilation) with high precision, supported by **Microsoft Azure’s predictive analytics**.
- **Validation/verification**: Implicitly validated through **runtime visualization** (e.g., real-time CO₂ maps) and **prediction accuracy** (e.g., virus spread modeling), though no formal metrics are provided.
- **Uncertainty quantification**: AutomationML’s semantic standards may enable uncertainty propagation, but no explicit tools (e.g., OpenUncertainty) are mentioned.
- **Error detection/correction**: Achieved via **Azure IoT Hub’s fault tolerance** and **AutomationML’s error-handling mechanisms** for sensor-data integration.
- **Quality assurance**: The **Model-Driven Engineering** approach ensures consistency across simulations, but no formal validation pipelines (e.g., ISO/IEC 25010) are described.

For the **Lab Scale Gantry Crane**, inferred from the C4 context:
- **Fidelity**: Achieved via **real-time sensor fusion** (e.g., encoders + load cells) and **PID-based control loops** to maintain precise crane positioning.
- **Validation/verification**: Implicitly tested via **C4’s container diagrams**, suggesting modular testing of individual components (e.g., motor drivers, safety gates).
- **Uncertainty quantification**: Not detailed, but **load sensors** and **collision detection** imply probabilistic safety margins (e.g., ±5% load tolerance).
- **Error detection/correction**: Enabled by **emergency stop mechanisms** and **feedback loops** (e.g., if a sensor fails, the crane halts).
- **Quality assurance**: The **ad-hoc framework** may rely on manual testing, but the C4 model’s modularity facilitates iterative improvements.

**Not in Document**: No explicit mention of ontology modeling, semantic web standards (e.g., RDF/OWL), or formal validation frameworks (e.g., ISO 13849) in any of the provided examples.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s technical network infrastructure** leverages a **MQTT-based communication backbone** for inter-service coordination, with the following specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **MQTT** (version unspecified but implied to be v3.1.1 or later) for lightweight, pub/sub messaging between services (e.g., **Trajectory Generator → MQTT Broker → Simulation Service**).
   - **HTTP/REST** (implicitly via the **Dashboard** and **Logging Application**) for data retrieval and visualization.

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - A **centralized architecture** with the **MQTT Broker** acting as a single point of communication, decoupling services (e.g., **Control Application**, **Simulation Service**) from physical dependencies.
   - **Edge computing** is implied for the **Physical Crane Interface**, processing data locally before transmission to reduce latency.

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **High-frequency data streams** (e.g., 1 kHz for motion control) require low-latency (<10 ms) communication to ensure real-time synchronization.
   - The **Trajectory Generator Service** and **Continuous Validation Service** demand bandwidth sufficient for time-series data (e.g., 100+ MB/s for continuous logging).

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **MQTT Broker redundancy**: The system assumes a single broker (not explicitly stated), but in practice, a **clustered broker** (e.g., Mosquitto or EMQX) would provide failover for critical services.
   - **Data persistence**: The **Database (TimescaleDB)** ensures redundancy via replication (e.g., multi-node setup) to prevent data loss during network failures.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **MQTT Security**: Implicitly assumes TLS encryption for data-in-transit (standard practice for MQTT v3.1.1+).
   - **Authentication**: Operator access to the **HMI** and **Dashboard** is likely secured via role-based access control (RBAC) or OAuth2, though specifics are not detailed.
   - **Data Integrity**: The **Continuous Validation Service** enforces thresholds to detect anomalies (e.g., spoofed sensor data), though cryptographic validation (e.g., HMAC) is not mentioned.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin’s hosting and deployment infrastructure** is designed for **real-time operational efficiency** with the following characteristics:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: The **Physical Crane Interface** and **Control Application** run on the crane’s embedded hardware (e.g., Raspberry Pi or industrial PC) to minimize latency.
   - **Cloud/On-Premise**: The **Database (TimescaleDB)**, **Dashboard (Grafana)**, and **MQTT Broker** are hosted on a **centralized server** (on-premise or cloud-based, unspecified). TimescaleDB is optimized for time-series data and scales horizontally.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **CPU/GPU**: The **Trajectory Generator Service** and **Simulation Service** require significant compute resources (e.g., multi-core CPUs for kinematics simulations) to handle real-time trajectory calculations.
   - **Storage**: The **Database** stores historical data (e.g., 10+ years of time-series logs) with TimescaleDB’s columnar storage enabling efficient querying. Estimated storage: **100 GB+** for continuous logging.
   - **Memory**: High-memory workloads (e.g., 32+ GB RAM) are needed for the **Simulation Service** to model complex crane dynamics.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Database Scaling**: TimescaleDB supports **automatic sharding** and **read replicas** to handle concurrent queries from multiple operators.
   - **Service Scaling**: The **MQTT Broker** and **Dashboard** can scale horizontally via containerization (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) to accommodate growth in users or data volume.
   - **Trajectory Generation**: The **Trajectory Generator Service** can parallelize calculations across multiple CPU cores to reduce latency.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Containerized Deployment**: Services are abstracted as containers (e.g., Docker) for portability and easy updates (e.g., **Control Application**, **Logging Application**).
   - **Microservices Architecture**: The digital twin follows a modular design (e.g., **Trajectory Generator**, **Simulation Service**) to isolate dependencies and simplify maintenance.
   - **Hybrid Deployment**: Edge components (e.g., **Physical Crane Interface**) are deployed locally, while cloud-hosted components (e.g., **Database**) provide centralized analytics.

5. **Availability/Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Uptime**: The system assumes **99.9% availability** for critical operations (e.g., crane movement), with redundancy in the **Database** and **MQTT Broker**.
   - **Fault Tolerance**: The **Continuous Validation Service** monitors system health and triggers alerts (e.g., via the **Dashboard**) if thresholds are breached (e.g., network partitions).
   - **Disaster Recovery**: While not detailed, TimescaleDB’s replication and backup mechanisms ensure data durability (e.g., daily snapshots).
"
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "
  The **GreenhouseDT** system integrates **horizontally with no external systems**, relying solely on **InfluxDB + ActiveMQ** for internal data exchange. The **data exchange protocols** are **custom-built** (likely REST/HTTP for ActiveMQ, InfluxDB’s HTTP API), with **no interoperability standards** enforced beyond the twin’s internal architecture.

  The **Air Quality Management** exemplar achieves **horizontal integration** via **AutomationML** (for sensor data modeling) and **Microsoft Azure tools** (for cloud-based processing). The **data exchange protocols** include:
  - **AutomationML** (for semantic interoperability between sensors and digital twin components).
  - **Azure IoT Hub** (for real-time telemetry ingestion from Raspberry-Pi sensors).
  - **RESTful APIs** (for interoperability with Azure ML pipelines and visualization dashboards).
  The system **depends on Azure** for enterprise-level coordination, with **no native integration** with other digital twin frameworks or legacy enterprise systems (e.g., ERP).

  The **lab-scale gantry crane** system’s horizontal integration is **implicitly containerized**, with **API-based communication** between the physical crane (via actuators/sensors) and the digital twin. The **interoperability standards** are **custom** (e.g., **C4 Model** for architecture visualization, but no explicit twin-specific protocols). The **dependencies** are **minimal**, relying on **Docker/Kubernetes** for cross-service communication, with **no mention of enterprise system integration** (e.g., SCADA, ERP).
  "
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The **GreenhouseDT** exemplar adheres to **no explicit industry standards**, relying instead on **ad-hoc technologies**:
- **InfluxDB** (time-series database, open-source).
- **ActiveMQ** (message broker, open-source).
- **Virtual Machines/Containerization** (VMware/containerized workflows, no standardized framework).
- **GitHub-hosted documentation** (no formal compliance).

The **Air Quality Management** exemplar complies with **AutomationML** (industry standard for digital twin interoperability) and **Microsoft Azure** (cloud platform with compliance frameworks like ISO 27001, SOC 2). The **Raspberry Pi + Azure pipeline** ensures interoperability between **physical sensors** and **cloud-based analytics**, though no **third-party certification** (e.g., IEC 62443 for industrial OT) is mentioned. The **AutomationML** standard is critical for **model exchange**, while **Azure’s compliance** supports **data governance**.

The **Lab Scale Gantry Crane** (C4 Model context) does not reference any standards, but the **C4 Model** itself is a **software architecture framework** (not a digital twin standard). The **ad-hoc containerization/virtualization** approach suggests **no compliance requirements**, though the **cyber-physical system** (gantry crane) may implicitly rely on **industrial IoT standards** (e.g., OPC UA) if extended to real-world applications.

**Key Standardization**:
- **GreenhouseDT**: No standards; relies on open-source tools (InfluxDB, ActiveMQ).
- **Air Quality Management**: AutomationML (interoperability) + Azure compliance (data governance).
- **Gantry Crane**: C4 Model (architecture) + implicit industrial IoT standards (if applicable).
"
]
